subject_tmpl: Request to Address the Village Board – {DATE} Meeting
body_tmpl: My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. I would like to sign up to speak during the public comment portion of the {DATE} Village Board meeting. My topic will be the condition of the sidewalks along {STREET} between {CROSS} and Washington Blvd. I understand there is a three-minute limit and I will respect it. Please confirm my spot on the speakers list and let me know whether I should arrive early to register in person. Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your email, {FIRST}. You have been added to the public comment list for the {DATE} meeting. Please arrive by {TIME} to check in with the Village Clerk's office. Speakers are called in the order they sign in.

subject_tmpl: Public Comment – Agenda Item on {STREET} Parking Ordinance
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I am writing to submit written public comment on the proposed parking ordinance affecting {STREET}. As a {DAYS}-year resident of the {ADDRESS} block, I believe the two-hour daytime limit will hurt working families who rely on street parking. I would appreciate it if this comment were entered into the record. If that is not possible, please let me know the correct procedure for submitting written remarks before the {DATE} vote.

subject_tmpl: Question About the {DATE} Board Vote on {STREET} Resurfacing
body_tmpl: Hi, I watched the {DATE} meeting on YouTube but the audio cut out during the vote on the {STREET} resurfacing contract. Could you tell me the final vote count and whether any trustees voted no? Also, when will the meeting minutes be posted online? I checked the village website this morning and they are not up yet. My name is {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. Thank you for your help.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, the {DATE} vote on the {STREET} resurfacing contract passed {NUM}-0. Draft minutes are typically posted within {DAYS} business days. I will email you a link as soon as they are approved.

subject_tmpl: Volunteer Interest – {DEPT} Advisory Committee
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I am {FIRST} {LAST} of {ADDRESS} and I am interested in volunteering on the {DEPT} Advisory Committee. I have {DAYS} years of experience in municipal planning and I attended the last {NUM} board meetings to learn more about the village's priorities. Could you tell me how committee members are selected and whether there are any open seats? I am available to meet at your convenience.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your interest, {FIRST}. The {DEPT} committee currently has one open seat. Please submit a letter of interest and a brief bio to my office by {DATE} and I will present your application at the next board session.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Board Meeting Minutes and Voting Records
body_tmpl: This is a FOIA request from {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}, {PHONE}. I am requesting copies of all Village Board meeting minutes from {DATE} through the present, as well as any roll-call voting records for ordinances related to {STREET}. Electronic copies in PDF format are preferred. Please confirm receipt and advise of any fees.
reply_tmpl: FOIA request received. Your case number is {CASE}. The statutory response period is five business days. We will notify you of any fees before processing. Electronic delivery will be to the email address from which this request was sent.

subject_tmpl: Concerned About Agenda Item – Rezoning Near {STREET} and {CROSS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here, {ADDRESS}. I just saw the agenda for the {DATE} board meeting and I have serious concerns about the proposed rezoning near {STREET} and {CROSS}. My neighbors and I worry about increased traffic and the impact on our block's character. We have {NUM} signatures on a petition I would like to present. How do I get on the agenda, and is there a limit to how many residents can speak on a single item?

subject_tmpl: Thank You for Addressing {STREET} Flooding at the Board Meeting
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I attended last Tuesday's board meeting and wanted to say thank you for personally addressing the drainage concerns on {STREET}. After {DAYS} months of trying to get answers, it was a relief to hear a commitment from the village. I appreciate that you stayed after the meeting to speak with residents. Please keep us informed as the project moves forward.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for attending, {FIRST}, and for your patience. I have asked our engineer to send you a project timeline by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Question on Variance Hearing Date for {BIZ}
body_tmpl: Good morning, I am writing on behalf of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. We applied for a side-yard variance on {DATE} (case {CASE}) and have not yet received a hearing date. The board meets monthly and we need to know whether we are scheduled for {DATE} or will be pushed to the following cycle. Could you check the status and confirm? We have a contractor lined up and every month's delay costs us {AMOUNT}.
reply_tmpl: Hello, case {CASE} is scheduled for the {DATE} Zoning Board meeting at {TIME}. You will receive a formal notice by mail within {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Request to Add Item to Board Agenda – Traffic Calming on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, my name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I represent a group of {NUM} households on {STREET} between {CROSS} and Marshall Ave. We are requesting that traffic calming measures be placed on a future board agenda for discussion. We have documented {NUM} near-miss incidents this year alone. I can attend any board meeting to present our data. What is the process to formally request agenda placement?

subject_tmpl: FOIA Request – Council Expenditures {DATE}
body_tmpl: Dear FOIA Officer, this request is submitted on behalf of a community journalism organization. We are requesting all expense reimbursements and travel records submitted by Village Board members and the Mayor's office for the period {DATE} through {DATE}. We also request any receipts or supporting documentation on file. Please respond within the statutory period. Contact: {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Board Meeting Livestream Was Down – {DATE}
body_tmpl: Hi Mayor, wanted to let you know that the {DATE} board meeting livestream on the village YouTube channel stopped working at approximately {TIME} and never came back. I rely on the stream because I cannot attend in person due to a disability. This has happened {NUM} times in the past year. Is there a plan to improve the technical setup? I would also like to know if recordings are archived anywhere. Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for letting us know, {FIRST}. I apologize for the disruption. We are working with our IT vendor to fix the stream reliability. The full recording will be posted to the village website by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: How Do I Apply to Serve on the Plan Commission?
body_tmpl: Good afternoon, I am interested in applying to serve on the Bellwood Plan Commission. I have a background in architecture and have lived at {ADDRESS} for {DAYS} years. Could you send me the application, tell me the term length, and let me know when the next open seat becomes available? I am also happy to attend a meeting as an observer first if that is recommended.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your interest! Plan Commission applications are available at Village Hall or on our website. The current term is {DAYS} years. We have one seat opening on {DATE}. I will send you an application packet today.

subject_tmpl: Opposing Proposed Liquor License Expansion – {DATE} Agenda
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I am writing to oppose the liquor license expansion on tonight's agenda for {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. My family lives one block away on {STREET} and we already experience noise and parking issues late at night. I cannot attend the meeting but I want this objection on the record. If the board approves the expansion, I would ask that conditions be placed on operating hours. Please acknowledge receipt. {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, your comments have been received and will be shared with the board prior to the vote. The village takes neighborhood input seriously in all liquor licensing decisions.

subject_tmpl: Speaking at the {DATE} Board Meeting – {BIZ} Development Proposal
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, {BIZ} would like to present our development proposal for the parcel at {ADDRESS} during the public comment period of the {DATE} board meeting. We would need approximately five minutes. Our team includes our architect and a traffic consultant. Will the board allow a brief slide presentation, and if so, what AV equipment is available? We want to ensure our neighbors have an opportunity to hear the full plan.
reply_tmpl: We look forward to your presentation. Please arrive by {TIME} to load your slides. We have a projector and HDMI connection. You will be allotted five minutes; please stay within that window.

subject_tmpl: FOIA – Agendas and Packet Materials for Last {NUM} Board Meetings
body_tmpl: This is a FOIA request. I am requesting the agenda packets and any staff reports included with the agenda for the last {NUM} regular Village Board meetings. I am interested specifically in items related to {STREET} capital improvements and any TIF district activity. PDF format is fine. My contact info: {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}, {PHONE}. Case number if already assigned: {CASE}.

subject_tmpl: Nomination – {FIRST} {LAST} for Citizen of the Year Recognition
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor, I would like to formally nominate {NAME} of {ADDRESS} for a village recognition at the next board meeting. {NAME} has volunteered {DAYS} hours this year organizing neighborhood cleanups along {STREET} and coordinating with Public Works. The {CROSS} block association unanimously supports this nomination. Please let me know if there is a formal submission form or if this email suffices.
reply_tmpl: What a wonderful nomination. Please submit a brief bio and a list of {NAME}'s contributions to my office by {DATE}. I will work with the Clerk to schedule a recognition at the {DATE} board meeting.

subject_tmpl: Question on Recent Vote – {STREET} Speed Humps
body_tmpl: Hi Mayor, I was surprised to see the speed hump proposal for {STREET} fail at the {DATE} meeting. I thought the engineering study supported the installation. Was the vote a final no, or can the item be brought back? I have {NUM} kids who walk to school on that block and this matters a great deal to our family. Any guidance on next steps would be appreciated. {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}.

subject_tmpl: Volunteer – Taste of Bellwood Planning Committee
body_tmpl: Hello Mayor Okonkwo, my name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. I have attended the Taste of Bellwood every year for {DAYS} years and would love to help plan this year's event. I have experience coordinating community festivals and I am particularly interested in the entertainment and vendor committees. Is there a volunteer application, and when does the planning committee start meeting? I look forward to contributing.
reply_tmpl: Thank you {FIRST}! We are excited to have community members involved. Please reach out to our Parks Coordinator directly to be added to the committee. Planning meetings start {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Intergovernmental Comment – Proposed Annexation Near {STREET}
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, on behalf of Proviso Township, I am writing to submit formal comment regarding the proposed annexation discussed at your {DATE} board meeting. We request that the Village provide {DAYS} days' notice of any annexation vote affecting parcels adjacent to {STREET} so that the Township can review impacts on road maintenance responsibilities. We would welcome a joint meeting to discuss boundary coordination before any final action. Please contact us at {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Agenda Not Posted 48 Hours Before Meeting – {DATE}
body_tmpl: Good morning, I noticed that the agenda for the {DATE} board meeting was not posted to the village website until late the evening before the meeting, which appears to be less than the 48-hour notice required under the Illinois Open Meetings Act. I am not making a formal complaint at this time, but I wanted to flag it. Could you confirm the exact posting time and advise whether the village believes it was compliant? Thank you. {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for the inquiry, {FIRST}. The agenda for the {DATE} meeting was posted at {TIME} on {DATE}, which is within the required window. We will provide documentation upon request. We take OMA compliance seriously.

subject_tmpl: Support for Proposed Ordinance – Vacant Property Registry
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor, I am writing in strong support of the proposed vacant property registry ordinance on the {DATE} agenda. The abandoned building at {ADDRESS} has been an eyesore and safety hazard for {DAYS} years. A registry with annual fees would give the village real tools to hold owners accountable. Please share this message with the board. I plan to attend and offer brief remarks during public comment. {FIRST} {LAST}, {STREET}.

subject_tmpl: Opposition to Proposed Sign Ordinance – {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, we are the owners of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. The proposed sign ordinance on the {DATE} agenda would require us to reduce our existing signage by {NUM} square feet. We have operated on {STREET} for {DAYS} years and our signage is essential to attracting customers from the commercial corridor. We respectfully request a meeting with staff before the vote to discuss potential exemptions or a phase-in period. We are happy to provide documentation of our current signage dimensions.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out. I have asked our Building Official to schedule a pre-vote meeting with your team before {DATE}. Please expect contact within {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Requesting Closed Session Justification – {DATE} Meeting
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, at the {DATE} board meeting the board moved into closed session. Under the Illinois OMA, the board is required to cite a specific statutory exception. The motion as I heard it did not clearly state the exception. Could you or the Clerk provide the specific OMA section cited for that closed session? I am not alleging any violation, just seeking clarification for the public record. Thank you. {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your question, {FIRST}. The closed session on {DATE} was held pursuant to 5 ILCS 120/2(c)(1) — personnel matters. The citation will appear in the approved minutes when posted.

subject_tmpl: Great Board Meeting on {DATE} – Keep It Up
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, just a quick note to say the {DATE} board meeting was one of the best I have attended in years. You kept things moving, every trustee got to ask questions, and residents were treated respectfully. The new format with printed agenda packets at the door was a small touch that made a big difference. Thank you and please pass along my appreciation to the Clerk's office as well. Proud Bellwood resident, {FIRST} {LAST}, {STREET}.

subject_tmpl: How to Submit Written Comments for the Record – {DATE} Meeting
body_tmpl: Hello, I would like to submit written comments about the proposed {STREET} infrastructure assessment but I cannot attend the {DATE} meeting in person. Does the Village accept written comments to be read into the record or attached to the minutes? If so, what is the deadline for submission and the word or page limit? I appreciate any guidance. {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Written comments are accepted and entered into the record. Please email them to this office by {TIME} on {DATE}. There is no strict length limit, but we ask that comments be concise. They will be distributed to the board and attached to the meeting minutes.

subject_tmpl: Committee Volunteer – Public Safety Advisory Board
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor, my name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I am a retired law enforcement professional living at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. I read that the Public Safety Advisory Board has openings and I am very interested in contributing. My background includes {DAYS} years in community policing and crime prevention. I am available to attend monthly meetings and would bring an independent perspective. Who should I contact to submit my qualifications?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your interest and your service, {FIRST}. Please send your resume or a brief summary of your experience to my office. The next advisory board meeting is {DATE} at {TIME} and you are welcome to attend as an observer.

subject_tmpl: Opposition to Budget Line Item – {DEPT} Staffing Increase
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I reviewed the proposed FY budget posted on the village website and I have concerns about the {DEPT} staffing increase totaling {AMOUNT}. With the village facing road and water infrastructure deficits, this seems like the wrong time to add administrative positions. I would like to speak at the budget hearing on {DATE} and ask that the board consider redirecting those funds to capital improvements on {STREET}. How long may I speak?

subject_tmpl: Request to Speak – Special Board Session on {DATE}
body_tmpl: Hello, I understand the Village Board is holding a special session on {DATE} regarding the proposed development at {ADDRESS}. I am {FIRST} {LAST}, a neighbor at {ADDRESS}, and I want to make sure residents have an opportunity to comment. Will there be a public comment period at the special meeting? If so, please add me to the list. If not, is there another way to get resident input into the discussion before any vote?
reply_tmpl: The {DATE} special session will include a public comment period. You have been added to the speakers list. Speakers will have {NUM} minutes each. Please arrive by {TIME}.

subject_tmpl: Requesting Copy of Approved Ordinance – {BIZ} Conditional Use
body_tmpl: Hello, I am following up on the conditional use permit approved by the board on {DATE} for our business, {BIZ}, at {ADDRESS}. We have not yet received a certified copy of the ordinance. Our lender requires this document for loan closing on {DATE}. Could you confirm the ordinance number and let us know when a certified copy will be available? We can pick it up in person or pay for a mailed copy. Case reference {CASE}.
reply_tmpl: The certified copy of Ordinance {CASE} is ready for pickup at the Clerk's office. If you need it mailed, please call us at the main Village Hall number and we can arrange that. Fee is {AMOUNT}.

subject_tmpl: Confused About Board Decision – {STREET} Stormwater Project
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I attended the {DATE} meeting but I'm still unclear on what was actually decided about the {STREET} stormwater project. The motion seemed to approve a study, not the actual construction. Can you clarify in plain language what was approved, the timeline, and when residents on {STREET} can expect work to start? A lot of my neighbors are asking and I want to give them accurate information. Thank you. {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Great question, {FIRST}. The board approved funding for an engineering study, not construction. The study will take approximately {DAYS} days. Construction, if approved, would not begin before {DATE}. I will post an update on the village website.

subject_tmpl: Public Comment – Against Proposed Tax Levy Increase
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo and Trustees, I am submitting this written comment ahead of the {DATE} tax levy vote. My family at {ADDRESS} is already stretched thin. A {AMOUNT} increase per household per year may seem small to some, but it adds up for working families. I urge the board to look for cuts before raising taxes. If there is a public hearing before the vote, I would like to attend. Please reply with the date and location. {FIRST} {LAST}, {STREET}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your comment, {FIRST}. The public hearing on the proposed levy will be held on {DATE} at {TIME} at Village Hall, {ADDRESS}. Your written comment will be distributed to all trustees.

subject_tmpl: Are Board Meetings Recorded and Archived?
body_tmpl: Hello, I am a new Bellwood resident at {ADDRESS} and I am trying to get up to speed on local issues. Are Village Board meetings recorded and archived somewhere I can watch them online? I am particularly interested in discussions about {STREET} from the past year. I work evenings so attending in person is difficult. If recordings are not available, are transcripts or detailed minutes posted? Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Welcome to Bellwood, {FIRST}! Board meetings are recorded and posted to our YouTube channel, usually within {DAYS} business days. Approved minutes are also on the village website. I can send you direct links to {STREET}-related agenda items if you let me know your specific interest.

subject_tmpl: Request to Form Ad Hoc Committee – Neighborhood Safety on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor, I am reaching out on behalf of {NUM} residents on {STREET} between {CROSS} and 25th Ave who would like to formally request the creation of an ad hoc neighborhood safety committee. Crime incidents have increased over the past {DAYS} months and residents feel they have no structured way to engage with the police department. We would like to bring this request before the board at the {DATE} meeting. Can you add it to the agenda or advise on the proper route?

subject_tmpl: FOIA – All Contracts Approved by Board in Last {NUM} Years
body_tmpl: FOIA request submitted by {FIRST} {LAST}, {BIZ}, {PHONE}. I am requesting a list of all contracts approved by the Village Board over the last {NUM} calendar years with a value exceeding {AMOUNT}, including vendor name, contract amount, scope, and board approval date. Electronic delivery preferred. If there is a cost estimate above the first {DAYS} hours, please advise before proceeding.
reply_tmpl: FOIA request received and assigned case {CASE}. An initial review suggests the responsive records may exceed the free first-hour threshold. We will provide a fee estimate within {DAYS} business days before proceeding.

subject_tmpl: Follow-Up on Board Commitment from {DATE} – {STREET} Lighting
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, at the {DATE} meeting the board unanimously committed to installing additional street lighting on {STREET} by end of {DATE}. That deadline has now passed and no lights have been installed. I want to follow up before I decide whether to raise this formally at the next meeting. Can you provide a status update and a revised completion date? I have photos documenting the poor lighting conditions if helpful. {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for following up, {FIRST}. I apologize for the delay. The light pole order was delayed by the vendor. Installation is now scheduled for the week of {DATE}. I will personally confirm completion with Public Works.
